dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3287 IS: Fair Allocation of Interstate Rates Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Cramer (for himself and Mr. Hoeven) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources

form.official-title: To prohibit the allocation of costs for certain electric transmission facilities to consumers in a State the public officials of which did not expressly consent to the transmission facility, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Fair Allocation of Interstate Rates Act.
Section 205 of the Federal Power Act (16 U.S.C. 824d) is amended by adding at the end the following:

subsection.header: Prohibition on allocation of certain costs

subparagraph.text: The term covered policy means a policy of a State, including any policy of a local political entity of a State.

The term covered transmission facility means any facility, line, equipment, or system used for the transmission of electric energy in interstate commerce that is planned, constructed, or operated in whole or in part to implement a covered policy.

paragraph.text: Except as provided in paragraph (3), a transmission provider providing electric service to consumers in 2 or more States may not allocate costs for a covered transmission facility to a consumer served by that transmission provider if—

subparagraph.text: the basis for construction or implementation of the covered transmission facility is, in whole or in part, to implement a covered policy of a State; and
the consumer is not a resident of the State the covered policy of which is the basis for constructing or implementing the covered transmission facility.

paragraph.text: A transmission provider providing electric service to consumers in 2 or more States may allocate costs for a covered transmission facility to a consumer described in paragraph (2)(B) if the State in which that consumer is a resident, or a designated public official of that State, expressly consents to such allocation of costs.

subparagraph.text: the benefits of a covered transmission facility accrue solely to the cost causers of that covered transmission facility;

any consumer who is a resident of a State that implements a covered policy that is, in whole or in part, the basis for constructing or implementing a covered transmission facility is a cost causer for purposes of subparagraph (A); and
any consumer that does not reside in the State described in subparagraph (B) is not a cost causer for purposes of subparagraph (A).

paragraph.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this subsection, the Commission shall issue such rules and regulations as are necessary to implement this subsection.
